title: Ensure that the cluster enforces Pod Security Standard  Baseline profile or stricter for all namespaces.

input_row.title: Ensure that the cluster enforces Pod Security Standard  Baseline profile or stricter for all namespaces.
input_row.description: The Pod Security Standard Baseline profile defines a baseline for container security. You can enforce this by using the built-in Pod Security Admission controller.
input_row.rationale: Without an active mechanism to enforce the Pod Security Standard Baseline profile, it is not possible to limit the use of containers with access to underlying cluster nodes, via mechanisms like privileged containers, or the use of hostPath volume mounts.
input_row.audit: Run the following command to list the namespaces that don't have the the baseline policy enforced. diff \ <(kubectl get namespace -l pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=baseline -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\n"}') \ <(kubectl get namespace -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\n"}')
input_row.remediation: Ensure that Pod Security Admission is in place for every namespace which contains user workloads. Run the following command to enforce the Baseline profile in a namespace: kubectl label namespace pod- security.kubernetes.io/enforce=baseline
input_row.impact: Enforcing a baseline profile will limit the use of containers.
input_row.default_value: By default, Pod Security Admission is enabled but no policies are in place. Internal Only - General

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:** 
- The audit command provided in the control description can be executed via the Kubernetes CLI (kubectl), which is a tool for interacting with GKE clusters. This means it can programmatically check whether the Pod Security Standard Baseline profile is enforced, thus making it suitable for automation. Furthermore, the control is standard and part of GKE governance, allowing for verification via the Kubernetes APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_cluster_pod_security_standard_enforced_baseline

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to query the current enforcement status of the Pod Security Admission controller.
- The audit command involves fetching all namespaces and comparing those that are enforcing the baseline policy with the total list of namespaces. The check can be implemented in an automated script using the following steps:
  1. Retrieve a list of namespaces that have the baseline policy enforced:
     - Command: `kubectl get namespace -l pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=baseline -o json`
  2. Retrieve a full list of namespaces:
     - Command: `kubectl get namespace -o json`
  3. Compare the retrieved lists to identify namespaces without the baseline policy applied.
- Pass/Fail criteria: 
  - Pass: All namespaces have the Pod Security Standard Baseline policy enforced.
  - Fail: One or more namespaces do not have the Pod Security Standard Baseline policy enforced.

This systematic approach allows for efficient checking of compliance with the Pod Security Standard, making it possible to regularly audit and ensure that security standards are maintained across GKE clusters.
